findings.attack_vector: An attacker can exploit the getTodoById endpoint by passing malicious SQL code through the id parameter. For example, executing GET /todos/0%20OR%201=1 will result in unauthorized data access.
findings.mitigation: Use parameterized queries for the database call, such as db.get('SELECT * FROM todos WHERE id = ?', [id], ...) to prevent SQL injection.

findings.attack_vector: In the createTodo function, an attacker could pass a malicious title that includes SQL code, allowing manipulation of the SQL statement.
findings.mitigation: Use parameterized queries for the database call, such as db.run('INSERT INTO todos (title) VALUES (?)', [title], ...) to prevent SQL injection.

output.summary: The current implementation contains critical SQL injection vulnerabilities in the todoController.js file when handling untrusted input from both URL parameters and request bodies. It is crucial to implement parameterized queries to mitigate these risks before proceeding to production.
